metadata.description: 前序遍历实现: Root → Left → Right. Recursive and Iterative (using stack) implementation. Used for copying tree structures. ### Example Code #### Recursive Implementation in Python: ```python class TreeNode: def __init__(self, val=0, left=None, right=None): self.val = val self.left = left self.right = right def copy_tree_recursive(root): if not root: return None new_root = TreeNode(root.val) new_root.left = copy_tree_recursive(root.left) new_root.right = copy_tree_recursive(root.right) return new_root # Example usage: # Constructing a simple tree root = TreeNode(1, TreeNode(2), TreeNode(3, TreeNode(4), TreeNode(5))) new_root = copy_tree_recursive(root) ``` #### Iterative Implementation using Stack in Python: ```python def copy_tree_iterative(root): if not root: return None stack = [root] new_root = TreeNode(root.val) node_map = {root: new_root} while stack: current_node = stack.pop() current_new_node = node_map[current_node] if current_node.left: left_new_node = TreeNode(current_node.left.val) current_new_node.left = left_new_node node_map[current_node.left] = left_new_node stack.append(current_node.left) if current_node.right: right_new_node = TreeNode(current_node.right.val) current_new_node.right = right_new_node node_map[current_node.right] = right_new_node stack.append(current_node.right) return new_root # Example usage (same as above): root = TreeNode(1, TreeNode(2), TreeNode(3, TreeNode(4), TreeNode(5))) new_root = copy_tree_iterative(root) ``` These examples demonstrate how to recursively and iteratively copy a tree structure in Python.

data_pointers.title: 前序遍历实现
data_pointers.content_preview: 前序遍历实现: Root → Left → Right. Recursive and Iterative (using stack) implementation. Used for copying tree structures. ### Example Code #### Recursive Implementation in Python: ```python class TreeNode: def __init__(self, val=0, left=None, right=None): self.val = val self.left = left self.right = right def copy_tree_recursive(root): if not root: return None new_root = TreeNode(root.val) new_root.left = copy_tree_recursive(root.left) new_root.right = copy_tree_recursive(root.right) return new_root # Example usage: # Constructing a simple tree root = TreeNode(1, TreeNode(2), TreeNode(3, TreeNode(4), TreeNode(5))) new_root = copy_tree_recursive(root) ``` #### Iterative Implementation using Stack in Python: ```python def copy_tree_iterative(root): if not root: return None stack = [root] new_root = TreeNode(root.val) node_map = {root: new_root} while stack: current_node = stack.pop() current_new_node = node_map[current_node] if current_node.left: left_new_node = TreeNode(current_node.left.val) current_new_node.left = left_new_node node_map[current_node.left] = left_new_node stack.append(current_node.left) if current_node.right: right_new_node = TreeNode(current_node.right.val) current_new_node.right = right_new_node node_map[current_node.right] = right_new_node stack.append(current_node.right) return new_root # Example usage (same as above): root = TreeNode(1, TreeNode(2), TreeNode(3, TreeNode(4), TreeNode(5))) new_root = copy_tree_iterative(root) ``` These examples demonstrate how to recursively and iteratively copy a tree structure in Python.
